reasoning:
Based on the FRED 'WLRRAL' series data, the most recently published value as of 2025-11-23 is 327,258 (for Nov 19), which is the absolute minimum over the last year. The precipitous drop to this level has been largely driven by the draining of the Overnight Reverse Repo (ON RRP) facility to near-zero levels (~$8B), leaving the total balance mostly composed of the Foreign Repo Pool.

For the 2025-11-30 resolution date (which will report the Nov 26 value), there is a strong likelihood of a near-term bounce. Nov 26 represents a standard month-end liquidity spike, historically associated with RRP balance increases, justifying a >50% probability of being higher than the 1-year floor of 327k. 

For 2025-12-23 (reporting Dec 17) and 2026-02-21 (reporting Feb 18), the observations fall mid-month and importantly avoid the massive quarter-end/year-end window dressing spikes (which hit on Dec 31). Without those seasonal tailwinds, the longer-term structural downward trend in federal reserve balances (driven by rate cuts and QT mechanics pushing balances lower by ~1,400/day) competes strongly against the possibility that 300k-320k acts as a firm functional floor for the foreign repo pool. Because of these competing dynamics, overall uncertainty progressively increases, warranting probabilities smoothly degrading towards 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends.
